title: Planning Evaluation
description: Guide your project by answering key planning decisions.

questions.question: Which is the BEST thesis statement for a blog about social media's impact?
options: "While social media connects people globally, its impact on mental health requires careful consideration"
questions.explanation: A strong thesis is specific, balanced, and arguable. It presents a clear position with nuance.

questions.question: What should come AFTER your thesis statement?
options: A personal anecdote unrelated to the topic
questions.explanation: After the thesis, present 2-3 supporting arguments with evidence to build your case.

questions.question: A strong conclusion should ___ your main argument and offer a final thought.
questions.explanation: The conclusion summarizes (restates key points) and leaves the audience with a final insight.

questions.question: Your project should demonstrate skills from...
options: Multiple modules: grammar, vocabulary, pronunciation, and critical thinking
questions.explanation: The final project integrates ALL skills from all 6 modules (8-13).

questions.question: Which is the FIRST phase of your project?
questions.explanation: Research comes first: choose your topic, read sources, and gather information.

questions.question: In the planning phase, you should create an ___ (outline/final draft) before writing.
questions.explanation: An outline organizes your ideas BEFORE you write. It's the blueprint of your project.

questions.question: Which grammar structures should you try to include in your project?
options: A variety: present perfect, modals, passive voice, conditionals
questions.explanation: Grammatical variety demonstrates your full range of skills across all modules.

questions.question: The evaluation rubric gives 25% to each of 4 areas. Which is NOT one of them?
questions.explanation: The 4 criteria are: Content, Grammar/Vocab, Pronunciation/Fluency, and Creativity. Length is not a criterion.

questions.question: "___ things considered, remote work has transformed the modern workplace." (All/Some)
questions.explanation: "All things considered" is a strong concluding phrase that signals a balanced final judgment.

questions.question: The most important quality of your project is...
options: Being authentic — showing what YOU can do with English
questions.explanation: Authenticity matters most. Show YOUR voice, YOUR ideas, YOUR English. Progress, not perfection.

title: Building the Outline
description: Build the key sentences for each section of your project.

sentences.prompt: Build a thesis statement about technology
sentences.correct: Although technology presents challenges, the benefits outweigh the risks.

sentences.prompt: Build a topic sentence for your first body paragraph
sentences.correct: First, the most important advantage is the accessibility of information.

sentences.prompt: Build a transitional sentence to the counterargument
sentences.correct: On the other hand, critics argue that technology creates dependency.
All things considered, technology is a powerful tool when used responsibly.

sentences.prompt: Build a sentence introducing your personal opinion
sentences.correct: From my perspective, the key is finding a healthy balance.
Have you ever wondered how technology is shaping our future?

title: Project Toolkit
description: Essential linguistic tools for your final project.

cards.back: In my view / From my perspective / I would argue that / It seems to me that...
cards.example: In my view, remote work has fundamentally changed the modern workplace.

cards.back: According to... / Research shows that... / The data indicates... / Studies suggest...
cards.example: According to a 2024 study, 70% of employees prefer hybrid work.

cards.back: Furthermore / Moreover / On the other hand / In contrast / Nevertheless / However...
cards.example: Remote work offers flexibility; however, it can also lead to isolation.

cards.back: For instance / For example / Such as / To illustrate / A case in point is...
cards.example: For instance, many tech companies have adopted fully remote models.

cards.back: Critics argue that... / Some might say... / It could be argued that... / Admittedly...
cards.example: Critics argue that remote work reduces team cohesion.

cards.back: In conclusion / To sum up / All things considered / Taking everything into account...
cards.example: All things considered, the benefits of remote work outweigh its drawbacks.

cards.back: It is worth noting that... / Importantly... / Significantly... / I'd like to highlight...
cards.example: It is worth noting that productivity actually increased during remote work.

cards.back: It seems that... / Perhaps... / It might be worth considering... / Arguably...
cards.example: It seems that the benefits vary depending on the industry.

cards.back: I would recommend... / It would be advisable to... / Companies should consider...
cards.example: I would recommend a hybrid approach that combines flexibility with collaboration.

cards.back: Have you ever wondered...? / Imagine... / Consider this... / Think about...
cards.example: Have you ever wondered what the workplace will look like in 10 years?

cards.back: Similarly / In contrast / Unlike / Whereas / While... / Compared to...
cards.example: While office work promotes collaboration, remote work enhances focus.

cards.back: Certainly / Undoubtedly / It is clear that... vs Perhaps / Possibly / It remains to be seen...
cards.example: It is clear that technology will continue to evolve rapidly.
